narration_text: Now we'll see how circuit switching works with Frequency Division Multiplexing and Time Division Multiplexing. Notice that with FDM, the available frequency band is divided into narrower bands, each allocated to a specific user, allowing them to transmit at the maximum rate of that band. On the other hand, TDM divides time into slots, allocating each call its own slot, enabling transmission at the maximum rate of the wider frequency band, but only during its assigned time slot. Let's think about this together, considering how these methods efficiently manage resources. I want you to focus on how these concepts apply to real-world scenarios, and we'll explore more examples on the next slide.
